text: The city of Mekka presents to the observer a spectacle of remarkable contrasts. Situated in a barren valley, surrounded by bare granite hills, it owes its existence entirely to the sacred character attributed to it by Islam.
book_name: Mekka in the Latter Part of the 19th Century

text: The inhabitants of Mekka may be broadly divided into several classes. First, there are the Sharifs, descendants of the Prophet, who claim a position of social pre-eminence. Second, the merchant class, which derives its prosperity from the pilgrim trade.
book_name: Mekka in the Latter Part of the 19th Century

text: The Great Mosque, al-Masjid al-Haram, serves not only as the center of worship but also as the principal institution of learning.
book_name: Mekka in the Latter Part of the 19th Century
